query_normalized: predicting the impact of processing temperature on the grain size of rolled aluminum alloys materials science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (d) as it provides the foundational theoretical framework (disconnection kinetics) for understanding grain boundary migration, which is the primary physical mechanism governing grain size evolution during thermal processing of polycrystalline materials like aluminum alloys.
